case_title: SYLESTER FLOWERS et al VS. KEVIN EGGE et al

DECLARATION OF WILLIAM WEBB FARRER IN SUPPORT OF PLTFS' OPPOS TO MOTION FOR ORDER OF RELIEF FROM DEFAULT: POS FILED BY PLAINTIFF FLOWERS, SYLESTER FLOWERS, HELEN FLOWERS HERITAGE FOUNDATION ALTA TIERRA PROPERTIES, LLC THE APOTHECARY EASTMONT TOWN CENTER, INC. RAMSELL CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT KEVIN EGGE'S MOTION FOR ORDER OF RELIEF FROM DEFAULT PURSUANT TO CAL CODE CIV PROC 473(B) IS GRANTED BASED ON ATTORNEY'S DECLARATION OF FAULT. ALTHOUGH DEFENDANT MAY BE RESPONSIBLE FOR THE DELAY IN EFFECTING SERVICE, HIS ATTORNEY HAS TAKEN RESPONSIBILITY FOR THE FAILURE TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT ONCE IT WAS SERVED. THE COURT, HOWEVER, CANNOT RECONCILE DEFENDANT'S SWORN STATEMENT THAT HE HAD NO KNOWLEDGE OF THIS CASE UNTIL HE RECEIVED A COPY OF THE REQUEST FOR DEFAULT, A STATEMENT SUBMITTED BY DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL WITH THE FIRST MOTION, WITH THE SWORN STATEMENT OF HIS COUNSEL NOW THAT THEY DISCUSSED THIS CASE ON FEBRUARY 3, 2007, WELL BEFORE MAILING OF THE REQUEST FOR DEFAULT LATER IN FEBRUARY. ONE OF THESE STATEMENTS IS NOT TRUTHFUL, AND DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL IS RESPONSIBLE FOR HAVING SUBMITTED WHICHEVER STATEMENT IS UNTRUTHFUL TO THE COURT. PURSUANT TO CCP 473(C), DEFENDANT'S ATTORNEY IS ORDERED TO PAY $1,000 TO THE STATE BAR CLIENT SECURITY FUND AND TO PAY PLAINTIFF'S REASONABLE COSTS AND FEES INCURRED IN PROCURING DEFAULT AND OPPOSING THE FIRST MOTION FOR RELIEF IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,725. RESPONSIVE PLEADING TO BE FILED BY MAY 31, 2007. NOTICE OF ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO BE SET THE SAME DATE AS THE DEMURRER. THE COURT WILL HOLD OFF REFERRAL TO THE STATE BAR TO AFFORD OPPORTUNITY FOR THE COUNSEL TO LOOK AT TRANSCRIPT AND PLEADINGS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT KEVIN EGGE MOTION FOR ORDER OR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT PURSUANT TO CCP 473(B)- DEFENDANT MOVES FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT ON THE GROUND THAT DESPITE SUBSTITUTE SERVICE, HE DID NOT RECEIVED ACTUAL NOTICE OF THIS ACTION IN TIME TO DEFEND. THE COURT THEREFORE TREATS THIS AS A MOTION FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP 473.5. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SUBMITTED THE DECLARATION REQUIRED BY CCP 473.5(B) THAT ANY LACK OF ACTUAL NOTICE WAS NOT CAUSED BY HIS AVOIDANCE OF SERVICE OR INEXCUSABLE NEGLECT THEREFORE THE MOTION IS DENIED. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-18-2007 Continued To Aug-10-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-21-2007 Continued To Jun-29-07 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Of Relief From Default Pursuant To Cal Code Civ Proc 473(B);
Off Calendar May-30-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- ATTY. WILLIAM WEBB FARRER, TEL. NO. 765-9100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. DAVID IRA KELVIN, TEL. NO. (510)865-0792 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT KEVIN EGGE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS - DEFENDANT KEVIN EGGE'S Motion For Order Of Relief From Default Pursuant To Cal Code Civ Proc 473(B) IS Granted based on attorney's declaration of fault. Although Defendant may be responsible for the delay in effecting service, his attorney has taken responsibility for the failure to respond to the complaint once it was served. The Court, however, cannot reconcile Defendant's sworn statement that he had no knowledge of this case until he received a copy of the request for default, a statement submitted by Defendant's counsel with the first motion, with the sworn statement of his counsel now that they discussed this case on February 3, 2007, well before mailing of the request for default later in February. One of these statements is not truthful, and Defendant's counsel is responsible for having submitted whichever statement is untruthful to the Court. Pursuant to CCP 473(c), Defendant's attorney is ordered to pay $1,000 to the State Bar Client Security Fund and to pay Plaintiff's reasonable costs and fees incurred in procuring default and opposing the first motion for relief in the amount of $5,725. RESPONSIVE PLEADING TO BE FILED BY MAY 31, 2007.
NOTICE OF ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE TO BE SET THE SAME DATE AS THE DEMURRER. THE COURT WILL HOLD OFF REFERRAL TO THE STATE BAR TO AFFORD OPPORTUNITY FOR THE COUNSEL TO LOOK AT TRANSCRIPT AND PLEADINGS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB/CK

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Or Relief From Default Pursuant To Cccp 473(B); Memorandum Of P/A In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Apr-06-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: David Ira Kelvin, Esq. 510/865-0792 for defendant KEVIN EGGE and moving for
relief from default
William Webb Farrer, Esq. 765-9100 for Plaintiffs and opposing party

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows -- DEFENDANT KEVIN EGGE Motion For Order Or Relief From Default Pursuant To CCP 473(B)- DEFENDANT MOVES FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT ON THE GROUND THAT DESPITE SUBSTITUTE SERVICE, HE DID NOT RECEIVED ACTUAL NOTICE OF THIS ACTION IN TIME TO DEFEND. THE COURT THEREFORE TREATS THIS AS A MOTION FOR RELIEF UNDER CCP 473.5. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SUBMITTED THE DECLARATION REQUIRED BY CCP 473.5(B) THAT ANY LACK OF ACTUAL NOTICE WAS NOT CAUSED BY HIS AVOIDANCE OF SERVICE OR INEXCUSABLE NEGLECT THEREFORE THE MOTION IS DENIED. Prevailing party to submit order. (301/PJB/CK Carol Karen, CSR #8189)
